_comment: smart_ac scheduler config -- copy to smart_ac.json, edit for your house, then sudo systemctl restart smart-ac
_rooms_comment: Room slugs match the input_boolean.ac_<slug> entities you create with setup_ac_input_booleans.py. Below are examples for a 6-AC house; substitute your own.
_day_min_comment: Which ACs are forced ON during the DAY period (between sunrise+morning_offset and sunset-evening_offset). Falls back to night_min_acs if unset. Set to [] to have NO baseline during the day -- lets the scheduler decide who runs based on day_priority alone. Useful if you want DEFICIT/CHARGE_BEHIND to turn EVERYTHING off (including bedrooms) so charging is maximised.
_day_priority_comment: Ordered list of DAY extras the scheduler considers, first-preferred first. In ON_TRACK mode, one extra is added if the corresponding room needs cooling. In SURPLUS mode, all needed extras are added.
_evening_comment: EVENING period = between effective_day_end (sun-based) and bedtime_hour. Family still up but solar gone. Target = evening_min_acs + evening_extra_required.
_unoccupied_comment: Classic no-one-home mode: relaxed targets, fewer ACs at once, daily rotation across priority to spread wear.
_vacation_comment: Stricter than unoccupied. Triggered by input_boolean.smart_ac_vacation_mode. Intended for extended absences where saving battery matters more than keeping the house cool.
_party_comment: Triggered by input_boolean.smart_ac_party_mode. Adds common-area ACs to the always-run set and lowers the comfort target.
_sensor_map_comment: Per-room temperature sensor entity IDs (or omit a room to use outdoor + heuristic).
_inverter_comment: Solar / battery sensor entity IDs. These names come from whatever integration exposes your inverter; substitute yours.
_battery_capacity_comment: Bank capacity used for time-to-empty math. Example: 840Ah at 51.2V nominal LFP = ~43 kWh.
